zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor Dennis於1515年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾代哈姆時，她的父親Sir William Denys45歲，母親Anne Berkeley41歲。她於1529年與William Lygon在英國格盧斯特斯爾代哈姆結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1567年9月24日在英國武斯特斯爾享年52歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭希迪 本 倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eleanor Dennis于1515年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾代哈姆时，她的父亲Sir William Denys45岁，母亲Anne Berkeley41岁。她于1529年与William Lygon在英国格盧斯特斯爾代哈姆结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1567年9月24日在英国武斯特斯爾享年52岁，埋葬在英国英格兰希迪 本 倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Dennis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Denis(e), Dinis(e), vernacular forms of Latin Dionysius, Greek Dionysios ‘(follower) of Dionysos’, an eastern god introduced to the classical pantheon at a relatively late date and bearing a name of probably Semitic origin. The name was borne by various early Christian saints, including Saint Denis, the martyred 3rd-century bishop of Paris who became the patron saint of France. From the 12th century onward, the popularity of the name in England (and of its cognates, mainly Denis , in the continental Europe) seems to have been largely due to French influence. The feminine form Dionysia (in vernacular English likewise Den(n)is) is also found, and some examples of the surname may originate from it.
English: from Middle English deneis, a variant of danais ‘Danish’, with the vowel influenced by Middle English denshe (Old English denisc). Compare Dence .

Irish (mainly Dublin and Cork): of the same origin as 1 and 2 above, sometimes an alternative form to Donohue but more often to McDonough , since the personal name Donnchadh was Anglicized as Donough or Denis.

variants.name: Dec
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aleksander, Andrzej, Casimir, Ignacy, Jerzy, Krystyna, Krzysztof, Malgorzata, Mikolaj, Stanislaw, Tadeusz.
Polish: from a pet form of the personal name Dionizy, Polish form of Dionysius (see Dennis ).

variants.name: Dennison
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Denis (see Dennis ) + -son. Compare Tennyson .
English (of Norman origin): from Anglo-Norman French deinzein, a burgess who enjoyed the privileges of those living deinz la cité ‘within the city’.

variants.name: Denny
meanings: English and Scottish: from Middle English Den(n)y, a pet form of the personal name Denis (see Dennis ). Compare Tenney .

English: habitational name from Danny in Hurstpierpoint, Sussex, recorded as Danye in 1343. Alternatively, the name may arise from Denny in Waterbeach, Cambridgeshire, but no medieval evidence with a habitative preposition has been found in the case of the latter.
Scottish: habitational name from Denny in Stirlingshire.

variants.name: Diniz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Augusto, Celso, Francisco, Luis, Ricardo, Rogerio, Alvaro, Anselmo, Antero, Carlos.  Portuguese Joao, Marcio.
Portuguese: from the personal name Dinis, a vernacular form of Dionysius (see Dennis ).

variants.name: Denes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Bela, Laszlo, Miklos, Tibor, Aranka, Balint, Endre, Ferenc, Lajos, Zoltan.  Jewish Imrich, Meyer.  Romanian Doina, Matei, Radu.
Hungarian (Dénes): from the personal name Dénes, from Greek Dionysios (see Dennis ). The surname Dénes is also found in Slovakia; compare 2 below.
Slovak (Deneš and Déneš) and Romanian (also Deneş): from the Hungarian personal name Dénes ‘Dennis’, or an adaptation of the Hungarian surname Dénes (see 1 above).

variants.name: Dennett
meanings: English: from a pet form of Middle English Denis (see Dennis ), or possibly from a pet form of Denise, as, for example, Denote, a female character in Piers Plowman.

variants.name: Dinges
meanings: German: from a vernacular form of the Latin personal name Dionysius (see Dennis ). Compare Dings .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Dings
meanings: Dutch and German: from a vernacular form of the Latin personal name Dionysius (see Dennis ). Compare Dinges .

variants.name: Dinis
meanings: Portuguese and Galician: from Dinis, a Portuguese personal name equivalent to Dennis .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Elinor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor Dennis was born in 1515, in Dyrham, Gloucestershire, England, her father, Sir William Denys, was 45 and her mother, Anne Berkeley, was 41. She married William Lygon in 1529, in Dyrham, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 24 September 1567, in Worcestershire, England, at the age of 52, and was buried in City of London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor Dennis est née en 1515, à Dyrham, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Denys, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Anne Berkeley, avait 41 ans. Elle a épousé William Lygon en 1529, à Dyrham, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 24 septembre 1567, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterrée à City of London, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hat 1529 in Dyrham, Gloucestershire, England William Lygon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 9 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 24. ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor Dennisが1515年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアディルハムで生まれた時，父Sir William Denysは45歳，母Anne Berkeleyは41歳であった。1529年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアディルハムでWilliam Lygonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1567年9月24日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアで52歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスシトイ オフ ロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor Dennis(이)가 1515년 영국 그루스스터실르 디람에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Denys(은)는 45세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Berkeley(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 1529년 영국 그루스스터실르 디람에서 William Lygon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1567년 9월 24일 영국 워스스터실르에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 시티 프 런던에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com William Lygon em 1529, em Dyrham, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor Dennis родилась в 1515 г. в местности дирам, глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William Denys было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Berkeley, 41. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Lygon в 1529 г. в местности дирам, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 24 сентября 1567 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 52 лет и была похоронена в местности сити оф лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con William Lygon en 1529, en Dyrham, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 24 de septiembre de 1567, en Worcestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultada en City of London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
